baseline_note: Worktree cut from main HEAD = 76d8d6e per HITL 2026-05-22 (0.6.0-rewrite closed; new work lands on main). Prompt-expected 8b5c81d was 5 commits behind main (all docs-only: 0.6.1 + 0.7.0 planning prompts, prettier-fixups, memex compat note, MD-lint fix).
commits: <sha>: docs(0.6.1): planning slice — design verification + plan doc + slice skeletons + canonical-runner template
plan_doc_length_note: Below the 400-600 line target; patch release is genuinely smaller than 0.6.0 minor — terse-and-factual per prompt tone guidance.

skeleton_lines_per_file._note: All skeletons in target 30-80 line range (pre-prettier); prettier-fixup may have nudged line counts ±2 but stayed in range.

design_amendment_note: No design-doc amendments required. Existing python.md L28-34 and typescript.md L30-36 caveat block-quotes already say 'defers to 0.6.1 (slice 12-TX-OPENREPORT)' — spec language stands as-is; the OPENREPORT-py/ts slices REMOVE the caveats when the bindings land. No ADR amendments either.
new_files_added: dev/notes/perf-canonical-runner-2026-MM.md (template; 0.6.1-AC012-measure slice fills numbers + verdict + flips status: template -> measured)
ac_id_acceptance_md_state: Not appended to dev/acceptance.md; HITL confirms first per planning-prompt § 4. Current max in acceptance.md: AC-070 (AC-068a/b under AC-068 family; new IDs slot as AC-068c/d).

dependabot_classification.pyo3_low_x2: version bump only (transitive); no fathomdb API surface impact. Existing Dependabot PRs on branches dependabot/cargo/pyo3-0.24.1 and dependabot/cargo/pyo3-0.28.3.
dependabot_classification.js-yaml_medium: version bump only; dev-dep only (TS toolchain); fathomdb does not use YAML merge operator. Existing PR on dependabot/npm_and_yarn/js-yaml-4.1.1.
dependabot_classification.idna_medium: version bump only; transitive Python dep; fathomdb does not process untrusted IDNA in any user-facing path. No dedicated PR branch — resolves via uv lock --upgrade-package idna.

axis_e_pin_verified: true — scripts/set-version.sh L88-92 explicitly skips fathomdb-embedder-api in set_workspace_dep_axis_w_versions(); --workspace mode L378-388 does NOT call set_embedder_api_version or set_workspace_dep_embedder_api_version; check_files L261-366 enforces explicit [package].version on embedder-api crate (rejects version.workspace = true). No regression from 0.6.0 GA shape. The AXIS-E-PIN slice's job is to LOCK-IN this behavior via a regression test.
parity_close_observation: TXPARITY-N slice family proposed by the planning prompt collapses to {OPENREPORT-py, OPENREPORT-ts} — the parity matrix has only one open ❌ row, and it is the OpenReport row. No additional TXPARITY slices needed.

verification.agent_verify: 2 BLOCKERs reproduce on main with no changes (env, not introduced by this slice): (1) AC-037 netns-deny-egress needs unshare -rUn (sandbox unavailable); (2) AC-050c removal-detect references '0.6.0-rewrite..HEAD' which fails since the local branch no longer exists post-closure. 0 violations.

verification.rustup_action_taken: Updated rustup stable from 1.94.1 -> 1.95.0 to clear pre-existing lint-step toolchain gap before re-running agent-verify.

Reproduces on main with no changes — pre-existing post-0.6.0-rewrite-closure regression.
blockers_encountered.recommended_action: Spawn a one-off fix slice that updates the verify-script's removal-detect baseline reference (e.g., to `v0.6.0..HEAD` or `origin/main..HEAD`). Out of scope for the planning slice per § Hard constraints 'NO source code changes', but orchestrator should land this before 0.6.1-AXIS-E-PIN (which wires a new test into agent-verify).

open_hitl_questions.options: append now (HITL approves text proposed in plan doc § Per-AC scoreboard) | wait until OPENREPORT-py/ts slices land and append at GA
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: Append at start of OPENREPORT-py slice — gives the slice a citable AC for its RED test (per TDD discipline).

open_hitl_questions.topic: OpenReport binding return shape — backward-compat strategy
open_hitl_questions.options: (a) NamedTuple/wrapper that's still Engine-typed and supports legacy `engine = Engine.open(...)` destructure | (b) introduce Engine.open_with_report (py) / Engine.openWithReport (ts) and keep Engine.open returning the engine handle only
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: (a) — symmetric, no new verb, closer to native shape. Patch-release contract favors additive-compatible shapes. Surface at codex review of OPENREPORT-py.

open_hitl_questions.topic: Fix B-001 (agent-verify removal-detect base-ref) in 0.6.1 scope?
open_hitl_questions.options: (a) yes — orchestrator spawns one-off WF-fix-style slice before AXIS-E-PIN | (b) no — log under 0.6.x patch chain follow-up
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: (a) — AXIS-E-PIN wires a new test into agent-verify; verify-script should be GREEN before then.
